verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2/TGN46 is a single-pass type-I transmembrane protein (437 aa; TM domain aa 382–402) whose dominant steady-state compartment is the trans-Golgi network. However, it is well-documented to continuously cycle between the TGN and the plasma membrane via CARTS carriers, with retrieval back via early endosomes. During the PM phase it carries its own TM anchor and exposes its large lumenal domain extracellularly — this is a documented minority surface pool with its own membrane anchor, qualifying as dual_localization. A 2026 preprint further confirms surface colocalization with viral particles and dynamin-dependent endocytosis via the extracellular luminal domain, directly evidencing cell-surface accessibility. The PM pool is transient and non-dominant, so verdict is contextual rather than yes.
